title: Timeline provider fetch and entry building
description: Implement timeline(for:in:) in Provider.swift. Detect absent credentials (CredentialsStore reads from shared Keychain) and return a single not-configured entry if missing. Otherwise call APIClient for top-5 items matching the selected ContentSource (getThreads top-5 or getArticles view:important unreadOnly:true top-5). For each item download+cache the hero image. Build 5 TimelineEntry values staggered ~3 min apart (entry i shows item i). Set TimelineReloadPolicy to .after(Date() + 30 * 60). On fetch failure use last-good cached entries if present; otherwise return an offline entry.
acceptance_criteria: Absent credentials returns a single not-configured entry; no APIClient call made
latestThreads source calls GET /threads with sort and limit=5
5 entries produced with monotonically increasing dates ~3 min apart
Network or 403/HTML failure falls back to last-good cached entries or offline entry

handoff_summary.completed: timeline(for:in:) implemented with credential check, API fetch (limit=5 for both sources), image download+cache, staggered entry building, last-good cache save, and offline fallback. Two new WidgetState cases added. Build verified clean.
handoff_summary.remaining: AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift still uses the placeholder provider — wiring AggregatorRadarProvider into the widget config and resolving the WidgetEntry/AggregatorRadarEntry type mismatch with WidgetViews.swift is a downstream bead.
handoff_summary.risks: WidgetViews.swift defines AggregatorRadarEntry (different type than WidgetEntry) — a downstream wiring bead must reconcile these. Offline reconstructed entries have contentItem:nil; the view must handle that gracefully.
handoff_summary.next_action: Wire AggregatorRadarProvider into AggregatorWidgetBundle and reconcile WidgetEntry/AggregatorRadarEntry type mismatch
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: WidgetViews.swift defines AggregatorRadarEntry (separate type). Any bead that touches WidgetViews or AggregatorWidgetBundle must reconcile entry types with Provider.swift's WidgetEntry.
handoff_summary.design_decisions: 1. Used `client.get("/threads", ...)` directly (not `getThreads`) to pass `limit=5` — avoiding a 50-item fetch when only 5 are needed; `getThreads` hardcodes limit=50 in APIClient. 2. Added `.notConfigured` and `.offline` as distinct WidgetState cases so the view can render appropriate UI per state rather than overloading `.error`. 3. LastGoodCache stores only `itemId` + `deepLinkURL` (not full Thread/Article) — hero images are separately persisted by WidgetImageCache; offline reconstructed entries carry `contentItem: nil` since model objects are not Encodable and model files are out of scope. 4. Target image size fixed at CGSize(155, 155) — matches small widget canvas; medium widget may need a wider size in a downstream bead. 5. CredentialsStore instantiated directly in the widget extension; it reads Keychain via the shared access group and falls back to the hardcoded production URL for baseURL (UserDefaults.standard is not shared between app and extension).
handoff_summary.test_coverage_notes: Tester should verify: (1) with no Keychain credentials — single entry with widgetState .notConfigured, no network request; (2) latestThreads — verify query includes sort=importance, show_dismissed=false, limit=5; (3) unreadImportant — verify query includes view=important, unread_only=true, limit=5; (4) successful fetch produces ≤5 entries with monotonically increasing dates 180 s apart; (5) policy is .after(now + 1800); (6) simulated network error with prior LastGoodCache data — returns offline entries with cached hero images; (7) simulated network error with no cache — returns single .offline entry; (8) 403 HTML response (APIError.cloudflareRejected) triggers offline fallback; (9) WidgetImageCache.prune is called retaining only current item IDs.
handoff_summary.known_limitations: 1. Offline reconstructed entries have contentItem:nil — view layer must handle nil contentItem gracefully (downstream bead). 2. AggregatorWidgetBundle.swift still uses AggregatorRadarPlaceholderProvider — AggregatorRadarProvider is not yet wired into the widget (downstream bead). 3. WidgetViews.swift uses AggregatorRadarEntry (different from WidgetEntry) — type mismatch must be resolved in a wiring bead. 4. Image target size 155×155 suits small widget only; medium widget may need different size. 5. baseURL falls back to hardcoded production URL in widget extension since UserDefaults.standard is not shared with main app.

conflict_risks: WidgetViews.swift defines AggregatorRadarEntry (separate type). Any bead that touches WidgetViews or AggregatorWidgetBundle must reconcile entry types with Provider.swift's WidgetEntry.

execution_history.summary: Implemented `timeline(for:in:)` in `AggregatorWidget/Provider.swift`. Added `WidgetState.notConfigured` and `.offline` cases, a `LastGoodCache` struct for App Group persistence, and `CachedEntryData` for serialized last-good metadata. The provider checks credentials, fetches top-5 threads or important articles, downloads/caches hero images, builds 5 staggered entries 3 min apart, and falls back to cached entries or an offline entry on failure.

guardrails.template_text:
# Developer Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Implement only the assigned bead inside the existing architecture and file scope.

## Corrective bead scope

If your `bead_id` ends in `-corrective` or `-corrective-<N>` (e.g. `B-abc12345-corrective`, `B-abc12345-test-corrective-2`), you are running as a CORRECTIVE bead. Your scope is **strictly limited to fixing the specific failure that blocked the parent bead**. Do not:

- Add unrelated improvements, refactors, or cleanup, even if you notice them
- Reapply previously-reverted changes (the operator reverted them for a reason)
- Expand the surface area beyond what is needed to unblock the parent
- Touch files outside what the parent bead's `expected_files` / `expected_globs` covered, unless the failure is genuinely in another file and there is no smaller fix

If you notice an unrelated issue while fixing the corrective scope: **file a separate bead** via `takt bead create` describing what you saw, but do not fix it in this corrective. The operator will prioritize and dispatch.

When in doubt about scope: prefer the smallest possible fix. The reviewer of this corrective will check that the scope was respected.

Allowed actions:
- Modify code, configuration, or local docs required to complete the assigned bead.
- Create sub-beads for discovered follow-up work that should be handled separately.
- Verify your `Swift` changes compile and import correctly with a quick syntax check: ``.

Disallowed actions:
- Run any test suite — not even a targeted `` run or any subset. Test execution is exclusively the tester agent's responsibility. Running tests wastes the agent budget and delays the pipeline.
- Redesign unrelated architecture or broaden scope beyond the assigned bead.
- Perform final review signoff in place of a review agent.
- Silently absorb unrelated test, documentation, or planning work that should be handed off.

## Memory

**Read memory at bead start.** Before touching any code, run three searches using `$TAKT_CMD` (injected by the orchestrator):

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace global
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>
$TAKT_CMD memory search "<bead topic keywords>" --namespace specs
```

Treat results as ambient context — apply relevant entries; skip entries that don't apply.

**Write to memory when you discover reusable project knowledge** — something that would have changed your approach if you had known it upfront, and is not already in CLAUDE.md or the guardrails.

```bash
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<concise fact>" --namespace global               # project-wide knowledge
$TAKT_CMD memory add "<discovery>" --namespace feature:<feature_root_id>  # feature-scoped
```

Required Handoff Fields:

Every developer bead **must** populate the following three fields in its output JSON. Reviewers and tester agents rely on these fields to scope their work without additional back-and-forth turns. Use `"N/A"` only when a field is genuinely inapplicable (e.g., a trivial rename with no design trade-offs); do not leave fields blank.

- **`design_decisions`** — Document non-obvious architectural or implementation choices made during this bead. Include alternatives considered and why the chosen approach was preferred. Reviewers use this to evaluate correctness without re-deriving intent. Set to `"N/A"` only if the change was purely mechanical with no meaningful trade-offs.

- **`test_coverage_notes`** — Describe what the tester agent should verify: new code paths, edge cases introduced, regression risks, and any known untested scenarios left to the tester. Set to `"N/A"` only if the bead touched no executable code paths (e.g., documentation-only changes).

- **`known_limitations`** — Call out constraints, deferred work, or known gaps in the implementation. This includes out-of-scope items deliberately left for follow-up beads and any areas where the implementation is intentionally incomplete. Set to `"N/A"` if the implementation is complete as specified with no known gaps.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON with concise implementation summary.
- Accurate touched files, changed files, risks, and follow-up handoff fields.
- Populated `design_decisions`, `test_coverage_notes`, and `known_limitations` fields in every response.
